channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rudy Giuliani faces contempt hearing as lawyers for election workers pursue $148M judgment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani showed up Friday for a contempt hearing to decide whether he has dodged and deceived lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment won on behalf of two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: Thomas has agreed to follow updated requirements on reporting trips and gifts, including clearer guidelines on hospitality from friends.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYPD presence increased at Trump Tower, Times Square
item.description: “While there are no immediate threats to our city at this time, out of an abundance of caution, we have heightened security.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Duchess Meghan returns to Instagram
item.description: (The Hill) -- Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, announced her return to Instagram on New Year’s Day with a celebratory post on the beach. The former actress Meghan Markle jogged across the sand in an all-white outfit with her hair flowing in the wind before writing 2025 in the sand.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Soldier in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans attack victims: What we know so far
item.description: Investigations continue into an "act of terrorism" in New Orleans' bustling Canal and Bourbon streets after a truck drove into a crowd, killing 15 people and injuring at least 35 more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver ID'd as Army soldier from Colorado: Source
item.description: Livelsberger allegedly served at one point at the same military base as the man suspected of driving into a crowd in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Meet the newest breed in American Kennel Club's lineup
item.description: Say hello to the latest dog in the American Kennel Club's lineup of recognized breeds. Or you might say “hej.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An aspiring nurse, a football star, a single mother and a father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — An 18-year-old girl dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a former Princeton football star suffered fatal injuries when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Multiple dead, injured after car plowed into crowd on Bourbon Street in act of terrorism
item.description: Multiple people are dead after the New Orleans Police Department reports a car may have plowed into a group of people on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, WGN 9

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Islamic State-inspired driver expressed desire to kill before deadly New Orleans rampage, Biden says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A U.S. Army veteran driving a pickup truck that bore the flag of the Islamic State group wrought carnage on New Orleans’ raucous New Year’s celebration, killing 15 people as he steered around a police blockade and slammed into revelers before being shot dead by police.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Honey browser extension accused of scamming influencers, users
item.description: In the the 23-minute video by user MegaLag, the PayPal property is accused of abusing the affiliate link system for its own profit.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel in 'intentional' act
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York employers must now offer paid medical leave during pregnancy
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Pregnant New Yorkers will be entitled to at least 20 hours of paid leave to attend prenatal medical appointments under a law that took effect Wednesday. Gov. Kathy Hochul said the policy makes New York the first state in the country to offer paid leave for prenatal care.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy the Baptist: Carter redefined ‘evangelical,’ from campaigns to race and women’s rights
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Before reaching the 1978 peace deal between Egypt’s Anwar Sadat and Israel’s Menachem Begin, Jimmy Carter managed months of intense preparation, high-stakes negotiations at Camp David and a field trip to the Gettysburg battlefield to demonstrate the consequences of war.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things that will shape US politics in 2025
item.description: The fallout of last month’s high-stakes election and recent battles within Congress are offering early clues of what will set the tone for politics in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Harry Chandler, Navy medic who survived Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor, dies at 103
item.description: Harry Chandler, a Navy medic who helped pull injured sailors from the oily waters of Pearl Harbor after the 1941 Japanese attack on the naval base, has died. He was 103.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: PlayStations for guns are offered up in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Dozens of cars lined up outside a New Orleans church parking lot this week as residents flocked to exchange firearms for video game consoles through a city-supported gun buyback initiative meant to improve public safety as Louisiana loosens gun ownership laws.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts condemns elected officials for intimidating judges
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts condemned elected officials who have intimidated judges and defied court rulings, highlighting them in his year-end report Tuesday as part of what Roberts called “illegitimate activity” that threatens the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Returning to the office? Here are some tips to find flexibility and help navigate the changes
item.description: Heading into 2025, thousands of workers face an unsettling reality: After years of working from the comfort of home, they must return to the office full-time for the first time since the coronavirus pandemic or look for new work.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, Fox 5

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: The department said it was working with the FBI and the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency and others to investigate the impact of the hack, and that the hack had been attributed to Chinese state-sponsored culprits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
